name: SSC CGL (Combined Graduate Level) Examination
description: SSC CGL is conducted for recruitment to various Group B and Group C posts in ministries, departments and organisations of the Government of India.

eligibility.educationRequired: Bachelor's Degree from a recognized University

examPattern.negativeMarking: 0.50 marks for each wrong answer in Tier-I; variable in Tier-II

subjects.name: Computer Knowledge
topics: Shortcuts & Basic Knowledge of MS Office

name: SSC CHSL (Combined Higher Secondary Level) Examination
description: SSC CHSL is conducted for recruitment to Lower Division Clerk (LDC), Junior Secretariat Assistant (JSA), Postal Assistant (PA), Sorting Assistant (SA), and Data Entry Operator (DEO).

eligibility.educationRequired: 12th Pass (Higher Secondary) from a recognized Board

examPattern.negativeMarking: 0.50 marks for each wrong answer in Tier-I

name: SSC MTS (Multi-Tasking Staff) Examination
description: SSC MTS exam is for recruitment to Multi-Tasking (Non-Technical) Staff and Havaldar (CBIC & CBN) in various central government departments.

examPattern.negativeMarking: 1 mark deducted for each wrong answer

name: SSC GD Constable Examination
description: SSC GD Constable exam is conducted for recruitment of Constables (GD) in CAPFs, NIA, SSF and Rifleman (GD) in Assam Rifles.

name: NEET UG (National Eligibility cum Entrance Test)
description: NEET UG is the single entrance examination for admission to MBBS, BDS, AYUSH, BVSc & AH and other medical/dental courses in India.

eligibility.educationRequired: 12th Pass with Physics, Chemistry, Biology/Biotechnology with minimum 50% marks

examPattern.negativeMarking: 1 mark deducted for each wrong answer (out of 4 marks per question)

subjects.name: Physics
topics: Motion of System of Particles & Rigid Body

name: JEE Main (Joint Entrance Examination Main)
description: JEE Main is for admission to NITs, IIITs, CFTIs and serves as an eligibility test for JEE Advanced (IIT admission).

eligibility.educationRequired: 12th Pass with Physics, Mathematics and one of Chemistry/Biology/Biotechnology/Technical Vocational Subject

examPattern.negativeMarking: 1 mark deducted for each wrong MCQ answer

subjects.name: Chemistry
topics: Organic Compounds containing Halogens, Oxygen, Nitrogen

name: UPSC Civil Services Examination (CSE)
description: The Civil Services Examination is conducted for recruitment to Indian Administrative Service (IAS), Indian Foreign Service (IFS), Indian Police Service (IPS) and other Group A & B Central Services.

eligibility.educationRequired: Bachelor's Degree from a recognized University
eligibility.attempts: 6 for General, 9 for OBC, Unlimited for SC/ST
eligibility.nationality: Indian (for IAS & IPS), Indian/Nepal/Bhutan for others

examPattern.negativeMarking: 1/3rd of marks for each wrong answer in Prelims

subjects.name: General Studies Paper I
topics: Current Events of National & International Importance
History of India & Indian National Movement

subjects.name: General Studies I
topics: History & Geography of the World & Society

subjects.name: General Studies IV (Ethics)
topics: Aptitude & Foundational Values for Civil Service

name: NDA (National Defence Academy) & Naval Academy Examination
description: NDA exam is conducted by UPSC for admission to Army, Navy and Air Force wings of National Defence Academy and Indian Naval Academy.

eligibility.educationRequired: 12th Pass or appearing (Physics & Mathematics for Navy/Air Force)

examPattern.negativeMarking: 1/3rd of marks for each wrong answer

name: RRB NTPC (Non-Technical Popular Categories)
description: RRB NTPC is conducted for recruitment to various Non-Technical Popular Categories posts in Indian Railways like Station Master, Goods Guard, Clerk, etc.

examPattern.negativeMarking: 1/3rd of marks for each wrong answer

name: RRB Group D Examination
description: RRB Group D exam is for recruitment to Level 1 posts like Track Maintainer, Helper, Assistant in various technical departments of Indian Railways.

examPattern.negativeMarking: 1/3rd of marks for each wrong answer

name: IBPS PO (Probationary Officer) Examination
description: IBPS PO exam is conducted for recruitment of Probationary Officers / Management Trainees in participating public sector banks.

eligibility.educationRequired: Bachelor's Degree from a recognized University

name: IBPS Clerk Examination
description: IBPS Clerk exam is conducted for recruitment of Clerical cadre posts in participating public sector banks across India.

eligibility.educationRequired: Bachelor's Degree from a recognized University

name: CDS (Combined Defence Services) Examination
description: CDS exam is conducted by UPSC for admission to Indian Military Academy (IMA), Indian Naval Academy (INA), Air Force Academy (AFA) and Officers Training Academy (OTA).

eligibility.educationRequired: Graduate from a recognized University (Engineering for Navy/Air Force tech entries)

examPattern.negativeMarking: 1/3rd of marks for each wrong answer

name: UGC NET (National Eligibility Test)
description: UGC NET is conducted to determine eligibility for the post of Assistant Professor and for the award of Junior Research Fellowship (JRF) in Indian universities and colleges.

eligibility.educationRequired: Master's Degree with minimum 55% marks (50% for SC/ST/OBC/PwD)
eligibility.attempts: No limit (for Assistant Professor); age limit for JRF

subjects.name: Paper II (Subject Specific)
topics: Detailed syllabus depends on the chosen subject out of 83 subjects
Available subjects include: Commerce, Economics, Political Science, History, English, Hindi, Computer Science, Management, Education, Sociology, Psychology, etc.

name: SBI PO (Probationary Officer) Examination
description: SBI PO exam is conducted by the State Bank of India for recruitment of Probationary Officers in SBI branches across India.

eligibility.educationRequired: Bachelor's Degree from a recognized University

name: CUET UG (Common University Entrance Test)
description: CUET UG is conducted by NTA for admission to undergraduate programs in Central Universities, State Universities, Deemed Universities and Private Universities across India.

examPattern.negativeMarking: 1 mark deducted for each wrong answer (out of 5 marks per question)

subjects.name: Domain Subjects
topics: Based on Class 12 NCERT syllabus for the chosen subject
Available: Physics, Chemistry, Biology, Mathematics, Accountancy, Business Studies, Economics, History, Political Science, Geography, Psychology, Sociology, Computer Science, etc.

name: AFCAT (Air Force Common Admission Test)
description: AFCAT is conducted by the Indian Air Force for recruitment to various branches as Commissioned Officers including Flying, Technical, and Ground Duty branches.

eligibility.educationRequired: Graduate with 60% in relevant stream (Engineering for Technical branch)

examPattern.negativeMarking: 1 mark deducted for each wrong answer (out of 3 marks per question)

name: CTET (Central Teacher Eligibility Test)
description: CTET is conducted by CBSE to certify the eligibility of candidates for appointment as teachers in Central Government schools (KVS, NVS, etc.) for Classes I-VIII.

eligibility.educationRequired: Graduation with D.El.Ed/B.Ed (Paper I: Class I-V, Paper II: Class VI-VIII)
